data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 109,000 USDT with a 2.56% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Sep 26, 2025, 07:33 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 109,000 USDT and is now trading at 108,973.28125 USDT, with a narrowed 2.56% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Backpack: S3 platform data set a new record in the second week, with weekly transaction volume exceeding US$10.2 billion, and XPL list airdrop is underway
data.description: Backpack announced on the X platform that the second week of Season 3 had set new platform records, including: $10.25 billion in weekly trading volume; $2 billion in 24-hour trading volume; $891 million in open margin; $592 million in open contracts; $581 million in loaned assets; and $401 million in custodial assets. Backpack also announced that the XPL chart airdrop will be distributed in the coming hours, and community users have reported receiving a certain amount of XPL tokens.

data.title: Binance Wallet now supports Plasma Network
data.description: According to official news, Binance Wallet announced that it now supports Plasma Network, and Binance plug-in wallet will also support Plasma Network soon.

data.title: Aster's 24-hour revenue tripled to over $16 million, surpassing Hyperliquid's.
data.description: According to Defillama data, Aster's revenue in the past 24 hours surged to approximately US$16.17 million, ranking second in the cryptocurrency revenue rankings, only less than Tether's US$22.08 million, and more than three times the revenue of Hyperliquid ($4.89 million).

data.title: Huang Licheng, the "Big Brother Maji", opened a 10x long position in XPL worth $2.333 million, and currently has a floating loss of $171,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, "Big Brother Maji" Huang Licheng (0x020...5872) opened a 10x long position in XPL worth $2.333 million in the past hour, holding 1.9 million tokens at an opening price of $1.31. His current floating loss is $171,000. His long positions in ETH, PUMP, and HYPE are also in a loss, bringing the account's total loss to $21.468 million. Over the past week, this address has lost $32.89 million in Hyperliquid.

data.title: United Nations Launches Global Dialogue on AI Governance
data.description: According to PANews, the United Nations held a high-level meeting on September 25 to initiate the 'Global Dialogue on AI Governance' mechanism. UN Secretary-General António Guterres addressed the gathering, emphasizing the platform's significance in focusing on this transformative technology. Guterres outlined the clear objectives of the global dialogue mechanism, which include establishing safe, reliable, and trustworthy AI systems based on international law, human rights, and effective oversight. The initiative aims to foster collaboration among governance systems, adjust regulations, reduce barriers, and promote economic cooperation. Additionally, it encourages open innovation accessible to all, including open-source tools.

data.title: R2 has completed the integration of 12 asset management institutions and officially launched the mainnet at 15:00 today
data.description: R2 Protocol announced the completion of integration with 12 global asset managers, including Securitize, BlackRock, Apollo, VanEck, Fasanara, Mercado Bitcoin, and Centrifuge. The mainnet will officially launch today, September 26th, at 3:00 PM (UTC+8), along with two flagship RWA products: - T-Bills Vault: offering an approximately 4% annualized yield, backed by BlackRock, VanEck, and Centrifuge; and - Private Credit Vault: offering an approximately 9–10% annualized yield, backed by Apollo, Mercado Bitcoin, Fasanara, and Goldfinch. A 22% platform subsidy is available for a limited time, bringing the combined annualized yield to 32%. R2 aims to bring real, institutional-grade returns to users worldwide without barriers to entry, realizing its vision of a transparent, composable, on-chain fund aggregator.

data.title: Peter Schiff Predicts Silver May Trigger Bitcoin Bubble Burst
data.description: According to Foresight News, economist and gold advocate Peter Schiff recently commented on social media about the current state of Bitcoin-related companies. Schiff noted that while many firms are emulating Strategy's business model, few have acknowledged that MSTR's stock price has fallen by 45% from its peak in November 2024. He suggested that Bitcoin financial companies are facing a harsh bear market. Schiff also expressed his belief that silver might be the catalyst for bursting the Bitcoin bubble, a shift from his earlier prediction that gold would play this role.

data.title: South Korean actress Hwang Jung-eum was sentenced to probation for embezzling approximately $3 million from her company to invest in cryptocurrency.
data.description: South Korean actress Hwang Jung-eum was sentenced to two years' probation by the Jeju District Court on Thursday for embezzling $3 million (4.34 billion won) from her personal agency to invest in cryptocurrency. This means she will not have to serve any prison time for the next four years, provided she doesn't commit any more crimes. Prosecutors had previously requested a three-year prison sentence in August, but the judge granted probation, taking into account her full repayment of the funds and her status as a first-time offender who had already made full restitution. (Decrypt)

data.title: Crypto Treasury Companies Face Potential Share Declines Amid PIPE Deals
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, crypto treasury companies that have raised capital through private investment in public equity (PIPE) deals are experiencing significant challenges, with their share prices potentially falling by up to 50%. Analytics platform CryptoQuant highlighted that these companies have faced major drawdowns, with share prices often aligning with their PIPE issuance levels. As shares trade above PIPE offering prices, investors nearing the end of their lock-up periods may be inclined to sell, further impacting share values.

PIPE deals, which allow private investors to purchase new shares below market price, have been a popular method for crypto treasury companies to quickly raise funds in a competitive sector. However, CryptoQuant noted that while these deals provide quick access to cash, they can negatively affect a company's stock performance. The increase in shares in circulation dilutes existing shareholders, and when PIPE investors sell, it creates an 'overhang' that pressures the stock price. CryptoQuant's analysis of Bitcoin (BTC) treasury companies that conducted PIPE deals revealed significant stock drawdowns, with share prices often gravitating toward their PIPE issuance levels. The report cited the example of Kindly MD (NAKA), a medical firm turned BTC treasury, whose shares fell by over half in a single day once its PIPE shares unlocked.

CryptoQuant observed that other crypto treasury companies backed by PIPE deals appear to be following a similar trajectory, with share prices declining post-PIPE deals. For instance, shares in Strive Inc. (ASST) closed at $2.75, down 78% from its 2025 peak of $13. Strive's PIPE was priced at $1.35, suggesting a potential 55% price drop from current levels as PIPE investors will soon be allowed to sell their shares, exerting further downward pressure. Similarly, Cantor Equity Partners (CEP), a blank-check company merging with treasury firm Twenty One Capital, priced its PIPE at $10, and its shares have fallen nearly 70% from their high to under $20, indicating a potential 50% price drop from current levels.

Other analysts have warned that even well-established crypto treasury companies are under pressure as the value of their crypto holdings approaches the company's value, potentially triggering a wave of selling. CryptoQuant emphasized that a sustained rally in Bitcoin is the only likely catalyst to prevent further declines in these stocks. Without such a rally, many stocks are poised to continue trending toward or below their PIPE prices.

data.title: South Korean Actor Sentenced for Misappropriating Funds for Cryptocurrency Investment
data.description:
According to PANews, South Korean actor Hwang Jung-eum has been sentenced by the Jeju District Court to two years in prison, suspended for four years, for misappropriating approximately 4.34 billion Korean won (around $3 million) from her personally held agency for cryptocurrency investments. The prosecution initially sought a three-year prison term in August, but the court took into account that this was her first offense and that she had fully repaid the amount.

The court's ruling revealed that about 4.2 billion Korean won was directly invested in cryptocurrency assets, while the remainder was used for tax payments. Legal representatives for Hwang stated that the resources involved originated from her personal earnings in the entertainment industry, and due to restrictions preventing the company from directly holding cryptocurrency assets, they were temporarily held under her name.

The incident has led to the removal of Hwang from various variety shows and advertising collaborations.
